note: Shared list locking the risky-pattern heuristic across runtimes. `risky` are patterns QRID_riskyPattern / CheckCharacter::riskyPattern must reject (config error, no compile); `safe` must pass through. The heuristic rejects nested quantifiers (bounded and unbounded) AND any repetition of a group that can match ambiguously — alternation, optionals, or inner quantifiers — with inner groups collapsed layer by layer so nesting cannot hide the shape. It deliberately over-rejects (a repeated alternation is refused even when its branches are disjoint). It is still a heuristic, not a proof: polynomial shapes like A*A* pass it, which is why the server keeps the match-time PCRE-error guard as a backstop and why input-length caps stay in place.
